title: Risk n Reward
description: Risk n Reward is full stack web web application featuring 10 mini-games built using .NET10
lessonsLearnt: Scope Creep is the Biggest Enemy
Refactoring is part of development, not a failure
lessonsLearntDescription:
The most simple solution is usually the best solution.
In this project complexity usually ended up meaning more time spent refactoring code
Breaking logic into smaller, focused components reduced bugs and improves code readability and quality.
Planning the architecture, even at a basic level, made a significant difference in how easy the code was to extend and understand.
This project was my first real exposure to Git and GitHub and fundamentally changed how I approach software development.
Adding features and is great and all but it has delayed my initial plan significantly. Now I know that some ideas are meant for future updates, post deployment.

A module of code performing the same instructions can be written in many different ways. Each way has certain benefits and tradeoffs. Sometimes optimising this block off code led to problems in other dependent blocks of code.
 This was frustrating in the beginning but I came to understand that this is part and parcel of software development, it is not a failure but an opportunity to rethink how I approach the problem.
projectIdea:
The idea came to me in the December holidays of 2025, I was trying to learn more 'advanced' coding concepts practicing in small isolated console projects.
This quickly becoming frustrating as yes I knew what OOP is but I wanted to understand how does this translate to a real project.
 Few days go by I keep seeing betting app advertisements on the television, in particular the Aviator game. I wanted to figure out the logic behind the game. I fired up my laptop and got to writing the code for my version of aviator called Crash and then the rest is history.

title: Coin Trace
description: Coin Trace a full stack web application that tracks personal finances.
lessonsLearnt: Code first approach to Database integration
projectIdea: This was based off a tutorial I found for ASPNET. I wanted to see how many useful features I could add to it and have a web based app i could use to track my on expenses as a student

title: PortfolioWebsite

tagline: Academic tracking web-based application for students
